era_context: The Roman Republic is in its final decade of relative peace before the outbreak of civil war. Political tensions between optimates and populares intensify. Mithridates VI dominates the eastern Mediterranean. In China, the Han Dynasty under Xuandi continues consolidation. The Parthian Empire controls Central Asian trade.

events.title: Lucius Cornelius Sulla serves as censor; consolidates political power
events.description: Lucius Cornelius Sulla, the future dictator, serves as censor in 95 BCE (or possibly 97 BCE; sources vary). This position gives him significant influence over the Senate and allows him to consolidate his political base. Sulla's rise to prominence marks the emergence of a military strongman who will soon plunge Rome into civil war. His censorship is a turning point in his political career.
events.certainty_note: Sulla's censorship is attested in later sources, but the exact year is disputed. Some sources place it in 97 BCE, others in 95 BCE. The dating is approximate.
cross_references: Leads to Sulla's rise to power (-88 to -78)

events.title: Han Dynasty under Xuandi; no major documented events
events.description: Emperor Xuandi's reign continues with administrative consolidation. No major events are specifically documented for 95 BCE in surviving sources. The year represents the broader context of Han stabilization.

sources.name: Han Shu (Book of Han) by Ban Gu

events.certainty_note: Chinese historical records do not provide specific documentation for 95 BCE.

events.title: Mithridates VI continues dominance in eastern Mediterranean
events.description: Mithridates VI remains the dominant power in the eastern Mediterranean. He maintains control over Pontic territories and exerts influence over neighboring kingdoms. His power is at its height, though direct conflict with Rome is still a few years away.
events.certainty_note: Mithridates' continued dominance is attested in later sources, but specific events in 95 BCE are not well-documented.

disconfirming_evidence: The dating of Sulla's censorship is disputed. Some sources place it in 97 BCE rather than 95 BCE. The exact year is uncertain. Additionally, the significance of Sulla's censorship as a turning point may be overstated in later historiography; it was one of several steps in his rise to power rather than a singular transformative moment.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 95 BCE is sparse. The only potentially significant event (Sulla's censorship) is itself uncertain in dating. Most information comes from later compilations written centuries after the events. The year is known primarily through its position within broader historical trends. Roman sources are particularly thin for this period; most information comes from later historians like Plutarch and Appian, who wrote in the 1st–2nd centuries CE.

graph_edges.note: Sulla's rise to power led to his march on Rome and civil war
